The fundamental challenge lies in the cost of energy: while technologies exist for low-carbon steel production, market prices for steel and electricity make these projects economically unsustainable. The German government has introduced subsidies to partially offset electricity costs until 2028,

item.title: Average U.S. Families Struggle to Afford Childcare, Study Shows
item.description: A recent LendingTree study highlights the growing unaffordability of childcare in the United States. Federal guidelines consider childcare affordable if it consumes no more than 7% of household income. However, with the average annual cost for caring for an infant and a four-year-old totaling $28,190, a two-child household would need an income of approximately $402,708 to meet this benchmark. The average U.S. two-child household earns just $145,656, meaning families would need a 176% income increase to afford childcare comfortably. State-level disparities are severe: in Hawaii, Nebraska, and Montana, families need more than 2.5 times the average income. Racial disparities are also evident, with American Indian and Black families requiring over 300% of current incomes to meet the affordability standard. Experts warn that these high costs contribute to declining birth rates, which fell below 1.6 children per woman in 2024. LendingTree recommends strategies such as maximizing employer ben

item.title: Ramaphosa Confirms South Africa’s Commitment to Strategic Non-Alignment Amid International Pressures
item.description:
On February 23, 2026, South African President Cyril Ramaphosa publicly reaffirmed his country’s commitment to a policy of non-alignment during the Armed Forces Day parade. He stated that South Africa would continue to pursue strategic autonomy and would not be drawn into the spheres of influence of rival global powers. His remarks come at a time of heightened geopolitical tensions and what he described as increasing external pressure, particularly from the United States.
South Africa, a member of the BRICS bloc since 2010 and the only African country in the G20, holds significant economic and political influence on the continent. According to the article, this strategic position has contributed to tensions with Washington. Former U.S. President Donald Trump is cited as criticizing South Africa’s participation in the G20 and reportedly did not attend the group’s summit in Johannesburg.
The article notes that China, Russia, and Turkey have expanded their commercial and diplomatic prese

item.title: Artemis II Launch Postponed to April Due to Helium System Issue
item.description: The launch of Artemis II, NASA’s second mission in the Artemis program, has been postponed to at least April 2026 due to an issue with the helium pressurization system on the rocket's ICPS second stage. Initially delayed because of hydrogen leaks during the first Wet Dress Rehearsal on February 2, 2026, the SLS rocket successfully completed a second propellant loading test on February 19 with no significant hydrogen leaks. Although NASA had announced a March 6 launch, the mission was delayed after a routine check detected a disruption in helium flow, which is essential for operating the RL-10B2 engine and purging fuel lines to prevent potential hazards. The problem mirrors previous issues experienced during Artemis I, highlighting the recurring technical challenges of the program. To inspect and address the helium system, the SLS must be moved back from Launch Pad 39B to the Vehicle Assembly Building (VAB), a complex and time-consuming procedure. Unlike Apollo-era Saturn V or shuttle l

item.title: Massive Exposure of One Billion Identity Records in Unsecured Vendor Database
item.description: A major data exposure was recently discovered involving a database containing roughly one billion sensitive identity records from at least 26 countries. The unsecured repository was linked to IDMerit, a global digital identity verification company used by businesses across financial services, fintech, telecommunications, insurance, and other regulated sectors. The exposed data included full names, dates of birth, physical addresses, phone numbers, email addresses, and national identification numbers, as well as identity verification logs and related metadata. Researchers found the database online without password protection or encryption, meaning anyone could have accessed or downloaded its contents. While there is no evidence that banks or other client institutions were directly breached, the exposure creates downstream risks such as identity theft, account takeover attempts, and targeted phishing attacks. The U.S., Mexico, the Philippines, Germany, and Italy were among the countries

item.title: Media Manipulation in the Ukraine War: Glenn Diesen at the UN Security Council
item.description: This video features Glenn Diesen, a researcher, presenting at the UN Security Council. He analyzes how media coverage of the Ukraine war may be biased and used to shape public perception, emphasizing the importance of critical analysis and the potential for narrative control by various actors. He argues that a deeper understanding of the underlying factors and motivations is necessary to accurately assess the conflict.

item.title: Google Patches Actively Exploited Chrome Zero-Day (CVE-2026–2441) in CSS Font Processing
item.description:
Google has released an emergency security update to address CVE-2026–2441, the first Chrome zero-day vulnerability reported in 2026. The flaw, rated high severity with a CVSS score of 8.8, was discovered by security researcher Shaheen Fazim and was being actively exploited in the wild. It affects Chrome versions prior to 145.0.7632.75 on Windows and macOS, and 144.0.7559.75 on Linux.
The vulnerability is a Use-After-Free (UAF) memory corruption bug in Chrome’s handling of Cascading Style Sheets (CSS), specifically within the processing of the @font-feature-values rule. In C++, the language used to build much of Chrome’s rendering engine, developers must manually manage memory. A UAF occurs when a program continues to reference memory after it has been freed, potentially allowing attackers to inject malicious data into that memory space.
In this case, Chrome’s internal font feature map could be modified while it was being iterated over, creating a race condition. If an attacker crafte

item.title: Tech Billionaires Limit Their Children’s Screen Time Despite Creating Popular Devices and Platforms
item.description: Many of the world’s leading tech billionaires, including Peter Thiel, Bill Gates, Steve Jobs, Steve Chen, Evan Spiegel, and Elon Musk, are publicly restricting their children’s access to the very technologies and social media platforms they helped develop. Steve Jobs, as early as 2010, said he limited his kids’ use of iPads. Peter Thiel allows his children only 1.5 hours of screen time per week, a practice mirrored by Snap CEO Evan Spiegel. Bill Gates delayed giving his children smartphones until age 14 and banned phones at dinner. Elon Musk acknowledged that not setting rules around social media for his children may have been a mistake. TikTok CEO Shou Zi Chew allows young children to use the app only under the under-13 safety settings, including vetted content and restricted posting. The caution comes amid growing evidence linking excessive screen use and short-form videos to reduced attention spans, poor cognition, and declining mental health in both minors and adults. Recent legisl

item.title: New Jersey Man With No Criminal Record Detained by ICE After Years-Old Deportation Order
item.description:
Abdellatif Hafraoui, a 60-year-old Moroccan immigrant who has lived in the United States for nearly four decades, was detained by ICE for 108 days after being arrested at Newark Liberty International Airport in August 2025. Hafraoui, who has no criminal record, was taken into custody while preparing to board a flight to Florida with his wife, Sandra, a three-time voter for former President Donald Trump. The arrest stemmed from a years-old deportation order issued after an immigration attorney failed to notify Hafraoui of a required court appearance. That attorney was later convicted in a federal immigration fraud scheme.
Hafraoui had previously held valid work authorization and worked as a concierge in Manhattan for nearly 20 years. After marrying Sandra in 2011, he pursued permanent residency through a spousal petition, unaware that the prior removal order remained active. ICE enforced the order, placing him in detention facilities in New Jersey, Louisiana and Arizona. He described h

item.title: Weekly Highlights from Moss & Fog: Art, Design, and Nature
item.description: This week, Moss & Fog’s Fog Signals roundup highlights a diverse mix of visually striking and creative content. Among the most popular posts, a photographer captured an incredible meteor streaking into an active volcano, a shot that combines natural phenomena with cinematic timing. Retro design enthusiasts enjoyed a feature on 1980s-style city logos by Czech designer Kostya Petrenko, bringing back a pixelated, analog aesthetic. Fashion lovers were drawn to Haiti’s Olympic uniforms, designed by Italian-Haitian designer Stella Jean, which showcase vibrant colors and cultural pride. In LEGO news, a new Monet-themed set transforms impressionist paintings into detailed brick constructions, offering a slow, meditative building experience. Architectural innovation was also featured with the Veluwemeer Aqueduct in the Netherlands, a fascinating structure where a road passes beneath a lake while boats sail overhead. Astronomy and nature photography appeared with stunning images of the NEOWISE c

item.title: Millie Bobby Brown Marks 22nd Birthday with David Harbour Amid Past Controversy
item.description: Millie Bobby Brown recently celebrated her 22nd birthday at Maison Close in New York City with a special group of guests, including her husband Jake Bongiovi, 'Stranger Things' co-star David Harbour, and other notable figures from the entertainment world. The event, which took place on a Friday evening, featured Brown in a striking white ruffled dress and her signature elaborate hairstyle. Harbour, who had been involved in a previous controversy surrounding alleged bullying claims by Brown, was seen arriving early and leaving late. The allegations surfaced months before the final season of 'Stranger Things,' where Brown had filed a harassment claim against Harbour. Despite the claims, the two stars appeared to maintain a professional and friendly relationship, posing together at the show's season 5 premiere. The birthday celebration marks a public gesture of unity between the two, with the guest list also including several 'Stranger Things' cast members and close family. The ongoing me

item.title: NATO Faces Internal Tensions and Blame Over Military Spending
item.description: NATO is currently experiencing a significant internal crisis, with member countries openly criticizing each other over military spending. Poland, acting as a voice for the United States, has publicly called on Spain, France, and Italy to increase their defense budgets to meet the commitments made at the last NATO summit in The Hague. Poland’s Defense Minister, Wladyslaw Kosiniak-Kamysz, specifically criticized these three nations for falling behind on military spending, stating, 'We need to do more, faster, and with more strength.' Germany has joined the chorus of criticism, with German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul labeling France’s efforts as insufficient. In response, French Defense Minister Catherine Vautrin emphasized that France has already doubled its defense budget in the last decade and will continue to increase spending. Meanwhile, Poland has emerged as one of NATO’s top contributors, allocating 4.48% of its GDP to defense, compared to around 2% in Spain, France, and Italy

item.title: Bernie Sanders urges urgent action on AI revolution, warns of unpreparedness in the US
item.description: Bernie Sanders has raised concerns about the lack of preparedness in the US regarding the rapid advancement of AI technologies. After meeting with tech leaders in California, Sanders called for immediate policy actions to slow down the development of AI, warning that Congress and the public are not ready for the dramatic changes it will bring. Sanders, alongside congressman Ro Khanna, expressed their fears that AI could exacerbate inequality and lead to mass job losses, particularly in industries such as trucking, fast food, and various white-collar roles. Sanders proposed a moratorium on the expansion of AI data centers as a temporary measure to allow policymakers to catch up. While Khanna disagreed with a moratorium, he suggested focusing on steering AI development with a focus on sustainability. The event was part of Sanders' ongoing efforts to challenge tech billionaires and advocate for a more equitable approach to the tech industry. The article also touches on the psychological e

item.title: Sam Altman Highlights the Energy Costs of AI and Human Development
item.description: In a recent interview, Sam Altman, the CEO of OpenAI, compared the resources required to train AI models with the time and energy needed for human development. He emphasized that while there is a lot of focus on the energy consumption of AI, human intelligence also takes years of investment, notably in the form of education and nutrition. According to Altman, it takes approximately 20 years for a person to mature and become 'smart,' which is equivalent to the years spent consuming food and other resources. He also discussed the importance of democratizing AI technology, warning that concentrating AI power in a few hands could lead to disastrous outcomes. Altman pointed out that India is emerging as a key player in the AI field, both in terms of adoption and innovation, and stressed that the country could soon be one of the largest AI markets in the world. OpenAI's strategy of 'iterative deployment' encourages the use of AI tools even when they are imperfect, aiming to empower individua

item.title: Small Business Owner Who Supported Trump Pleads for the Release of Detained Fiancée Facing Deportation
item.description: Wayne DeMario, a Miami-Dade small business owner, is making an emotional plea to President Donald Trump after his fiancée of 20 years, Yamile Alcantu, was detained by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). Alcantu, a Cuban national who has lived in the U.S. for 25 years, was detained during a routine check-in with immigration authorities in June 2025. The couple never expected any issues with her immigration status, as Alcantu had been following the required check-ins annually without trouble. The detention, however, stems from an old traffic stop in 2008, where three Xanax pills were found in her purse, leading to a deportation order despite her clean record since then. Alcantu was taken in shackles to a detention center and could face deportation back to Cuba, a country currently facing economic struggles. The situation is financially straining for DeMario, who is asking for donations to cover the legal costs through a GoFundMe page. The couple had long been supporters of Tr

item.title: Impact of Technology in Schools: How Laptops and Tablets Are Affecting Gen Z’s Cognitive Abilities
item.description: In 2002, Maine became the first state to introduce a statewide laptop program, aiming to provide students with greater access to technology and the internet. However, decades later, experts argue that the decision to replace textbooks with digital devices has had unintended consequences. Despite massive investments—$30 billion spent nationwide in 2024 on laptops and tablets for students—Gen Z is performing worse on cognitive assessments and standardized tests compared to previous generations. Studies show a direct correlation between increased screen time in the classroom and lower academic performance. Neuroscientists suggest that technology, particularly when used indiscriminately in education, may hinder the development of critical cognitive skills, such as attention and memory. Social media and gaming apps, designed to be addictive, further disrupt learning and focus. Some educators and experts propose solutions like stricter regulations on technology in schools, as well as more re

item.title: New Rogue One Prequel Comics to Explore Characters from the Film
item.description: Marvel Comics, in collaboration with Lucasfilm, is set to release a series of prequel comics based on the characters from 'Rogue One: A Star Wars Story.' These new comics follow the success of the 'Andor' TV show, which reintroduced audiences to the Rebel Alliance's fight against the Empire. The five one-shots will focus on main characters like Cassian Andor, Jyn Erso, Chirrut Îmwe, Baze Malbus, Saw Gerrera, and even Darth Vader, delving into their backstories before the events of the 2016 film. 'Rogue One' has long been celebrated for its strong ensemble cast, and these comics aim to fill in the gaps in their pasts. Each comic will be illustrated by top artists including David Marquez, and the first in the series is scheduled to release in May 2026, with subsequent issues arriving throughout the year. Fans can look forward to stories that explore espionage missions, personal struggles, and key moments from the characters' pasts that shaped their roles in the Rebellion. This series pro

item.title: The Gleiwitz Radio Tower: The Site of a Fake Attack That Triggered WWII
item.description: The Gleiwitz radio station, located near the German-Polish border, is known for being the site of a dramatic incident that led to the outbreak of World War II. On August 31, 1939, Nazi operatives staged a false flag attack, using Polish soldiers' uniforms to create the illusion of a Polish invasion. They broadcasted a fake message calling for an uprising against Germany. Despite its lack of credibility, this staged incident provided Adolf Hitler with the pretext to launch an invasion of Poland the following day, marking the start of the war in Europe. The radio tower itself, which was constructed in 1935, is still standing and is the tallest wooden structure in the world. Originally used for radio transmission, the tower continues to serve for telecommunications today. The incident, now referred to as the Gleiwitz Incident, is remembered as a key moment in the lead-up to WWII. The tower is now a museum, preserving the history of this infamous event, while the city of Gliwice has underg

item.title: Satellite Imagery Reveals U.S. Military Buildup in the Gulf Region Amid Tensions with Iran
item.description: As U.S. and Iranian diplomats engage in nuclear talks in Geneva, satellite images are revealing increasing military activity across the Gulf. Commercial satellites from Planet Labs have captured detailed images of U.S. forces in the region, including the repositioning of Patriot missiles and the presence of warplanes and submarines. Tensions are rising as U.S. President Donald Trump gives an ultimatum to Iran, warning of 'bad things' if no agreement is reached. Meanwhile, the Iranian military has fortified positions and deployed long-range missiles, signaling a defensive posture. The buildup of forces, which includes the deployment of aircraft carriers and electronic warfare planes, could signal preparations for military action. Iranian officials, including President Massoud Pezeshkian, maintain that they do not seek war but are ready to defend their sovereignty. In response to the threat of U.S. attacks, Iran has highlighted the development of advanced weapons, including the Hoot torp

item.title: Trial of Captain of 'Ghost Fleet' Ship to Take Place Tomorrow
item.description: Tomorrow, a new trial will take place for the captain of the Boracay, a ship accused of being part of the 'Russian ghost fleet.' Initially, accusations against the captain revolved around economic sanctions, 'false flag' claims, and spying allegations, but now he faces charges of 'disobedience.' The French Navy boarded the Boracay last September off the coast of Brittany. At the time of the boarding, the ship flew the flag of Benin but later switched to the Russian flag. The prosecution has omitted mention of the flag in its charges. The captain, Chen Zhangjie, is a 39-year-old Chinese national who will be defended by Henri de Richemont, a prominent lawyer. The case hinges on whether French courts have jurisdiction, as international law (UNCLOS) suggests only Beninese or Chinese courts can try the case. This follows similar precedents where courts in Finland and other countries have rejected claims based on international maritime law. The captain faces one year in prison and a fine of

item.title: U.S. Expands Immigrant Detention Capacity Amid Rising Deportations
item.description: The United States is accelerating plans to expand its immigrant detention centers under the Trump administration. The government is exploring the creation of new facilities and increasing the capacity of existing ones to significantly accommodate more detainees awaiting deportation. Some facilities may surpass current capacity limits, utilizing industrial spaces such as former warehouses. The goal is to handle the rising number of detained individuals as part of expulsion procedures. These centers will be under the supervision of institutions like ICE and the Department of Homeland Security, and will aid in mass deportations. Some plans in Texas and other border states aim to house up to 10,000 individuals in a single facility. The detention of immigrants by U.S. immigration authorities has increased in recent months, with thousands detained daily. The Trump administration's approach to immigration is focused on tighter border control, accelerating deportations, and expanding detention

item.title: Billionaire Criticism of Spain's Prime Minister Highlights Success in Workers' Rights and Economic Growth
item.description: When Elon Musk recently branded Spain’s Prime Minister, Pedro Sánchez, a 'true fascist totalitarian', it sparked questions about why billionaires seem to despise his leadership. Sánchez has implemented policies that prioritize ordinary citizens' welfare, from raising Spain's minimum wage by over 60%, to reducing temporary job contracts by over 50%. Spain's economy has flourished under Sánchez, making it one of Europe's fastest-growing economies. Rather than following neoliberal policies, Sánchez has chosen to tackle inequality by securing workers' rights and integrating migrants into the workforce. His government has also focused on international human rights and resisted pressure from powerful figures like Donald Trump. Despite being criticized by the billionaire class, Sánchez's policies have positively impacted Spain’s economy and the lives of its people. The fact that the wealthiest individuals are attacking his approach suggests that Sánchez is on the right path. The shift towards

item.title: Drones Revolutionize Winter Olympics Coverage with Dynamic, Close-Up Action Shots
item.description: The 2026 Winter Olympics in Milan and Cortina marked a turning point in sports broadcasting with the introduction of FPV drones, offering an immersive experience for viewers. Unlike traditional, distant camera shots from helicopters, these drones dive alongside athletes, providing real-time action perspectives from the slopes to the ice. Skiers, snowboarders, and bobsledders are now filmed with incredible agility, offering viewers a sense of speed and intensity never seen before. The drones captured the steepness of the slopes, the tightness of turns, and the precision required from athletes, creating a more visceral connection to the events. Extensive testing was done to ensure that the drones did not interfere with athletes' performances, and the result was stunning footage that brings viewers closer to the action while respecting the safety of the competitors. This technological leap honors the athletes' dedication and the high-stakes nature of winter sports.

item.title: Tucker Carlson Criticizes Israel as the Most Violent Region, Expresses Concerns About Safety During Visit
item.description: Tucker Carlson, the American podcaster and former Fox News host, recently sparked controversy with statements on his podcast, calling Israel 'the most violent region in the world.' Carlson described his concerns about the safety of his flight to Israel, fearing that the Israeli military might mistakenly identify his private plane as an Iranian drone. He explained that he intended to share his flight information with Israeli authorities to prevent such a mix-up. Carlson's comments reflect his broader criticism of the region's militarization, stating that Israel's history of wars on multiple fronts and its armed populace contribute to its reputation for violence. Furthermore, Carlson recounted a tense experience in Tel Aviv where, after meeting with U.S. Ambassador to the Occupied Territories Mike Huckabee, Israeli officials confiscated their passports and interrogated his team. Despite initial resistance, Israel allowed Carlson entry to avoid diplomatic fallout. This incident highlights

item.title: Seth Meyers Predicts Trump Would Use Alien Life Announcement as Distraction from Epstein Files
item.description: Seth Meyers, in a July episode of 'Late Night with Seth Meyers', predicted that Donald Trump would announce the existence of extraterrestrial life to distract from the ongoing controversy surrounding Jeffrey Epstein. Meyers speculated that the release of Epstein-related files, which could implicate Trump, would trigger the president's attempt to shift the narrative. His prediction came true when, in late September, Trump ordered U.S. government agencies to begin releasing classified information on UFOs and extraterrestrial life. Trump's announcement followed accusations that former President Obama had declassified some alien-related documents and statements. Despite his order, Trump has not confirmed the existence of aliens but instead suggested that Obama’s comments had sparked public interest. The White House defended Trump's actions, claiming he had done more for Epstein's victims than anyone else. Trump's relationship with Epstein has been scrutinized, although he denies any wrongd

item.title: ICE Expands Detention Camps by Converting Warehouses into Immigrant Holding Facilities
item.description: The Trump administration has begun purchasing large industrial warehouses across the U.S. with the intent to convert them into mass detention centers for immigrants. These spaces, typically designed for storing goods, are being repurposed to house thousands of people, raising concerns about the inhumane conditions they will endure. Some of these warehouses are massive, with capacities of up to 9,000 individuals. While ICE currently holds around 70,000 people in 224 detention centers, this new approach reflects a significant expansion of detention capabilities, funded by a $45bn budget. The facilities’ poor design for human habitation, combined with reports of mistreatment, illness, and abuse at existing detention centers, has led to widespread protests. Local communities, often with the support of Democratic officials, are organizing against the conversion of warehouses into camps. Despite this growing resistance, the Trump administration is pushing forward with its mass deportation ag

item.title: Leqaa Kordia's Struggle in ICE Custody: A Year of Neglect and Cruelty
item.description: Leqaa Kordia, a 33-year-old Palestinian woman, has been detained in an ICE facility for almost a year. Arrested after protesting for Palestinian rights, she has faced severe medical neglect and inhumane conditions in custody, including overcrowding, malnutrition, and lack of access to necessary healthcare. Her arrest came after she participated in a protest advocating for a ceasefire in Gaza, but the U.S. government claims it was for a visa issue. Despite being granted bond twice and receiving a recommendation for release from a federal judge, bureaucratic loopholes have kept her imprisoned. Leqaa's condition deteriorated after months of deprivation and inadequate medical care, leading to her collapse in February 2026. The lack of transparency surrounding her medical condition and disappearance for nearly a day after her collapse has raised alarms. Two Texas lawmakers have condemned the treatment she’s received, calling it intentional cruelty. Leqaa's story is a stark example of the U.

item.title: Small Business Owner Appeals to Trump for Release of Detained Fiancé
item.description: Wayne DeMario, a small business owner who voted for Donald Trump, is making a public plea to the President for help after his fiancé, Yamile Alcantu, was detained by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). Alcantu, a Cuban immigrant, had been living in the U.S. for 25 years and had been checking in with ICE regularly. However, after a minor legal incident years ago, she received a deportation order. Despite this, DeMario never expected her detention, especially under Trump's crackdown on immigration. Now held in Louisiana, Alcantu is facing potential deportation back to Cuba, a country experiencing significant instability. DeMario, who has been struggling emotionally and financially, launched a GoFundMe page to raise money for legal fees. He describes Alcantu as the love of his life and his best friend, emphasizing the toll this has taken on their lives. The situation highlights the personal consequences of current immigration policies on families.

item.title: UK Couple Receives $200,000 Medical Bill Following Premature Birth in the US
item.description: UK folk musicians Issy Ferris and Archie Sylvester faced a $200,000 medical bill after their son, Lucky, was born prematurely at 33 weeks during their tour in Nashville. Issy experienced severe symptoms initially mistaken for food poisoning, but doctors diagnosed her with pre-eclampsia, a serious pregnancy complication. An emergency C-section was performed, and Lucky was immediately admitted to the neonatal intensive care unit, where he remained for three weeks. The couple had travel insurance covering pregnancy-related issues, including pre-eclampsia, but insurers initially refused to cover the full cost, arguing that only treatment for Issy's condition would be reimbursed, not the necessary birth. After ten months of legal battles and hiring a lawyer, the insurance company finally paid both the medical bill and legal fees, preventing the couple from having to sell their home in Somerset. Throughout this ordeal, the couple balanced hospital visits with continuing their music career, l

item.title: Supreme Court Invalidates Trump’s Emergency Tariffs, $175 Billion Could Be Refunded
item.description: The Supreme Court of the United States has ruled that former President Donald Trump exceeded his authority by imposing sweeping emergency tariffs under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA). Economists from the Penn-Wharton Budget Model estimate that over $175 billion in U.S. tariff revenue could now be eligible for refunds. This figure is based on detailed import and tariff data, covering roughly 11,000 product categories from 233 countries. The decision marks a significant legal setback for Trump and may trigger a surge of refund claims from affected companies, with potential impacts on the U.S. Treasury. While Trump has highlighted the revenue from tariffs, which the Congressional Budget Office projected at $300 billion annually, the Supreme Court’s ruling opens the door for substantial reimbursements. In response, Trump immediately replaced the invalidated tariffs with a temporary 10% global import duty for 150 days and initiated trade investigations under other l

item.title: Feuerwasser Wood-Fired Garden Shower: A Simple, Elemental Outdoor Bath
item.description: The Feuerwasser Garden Shower offers a unique outdoor bathing experience that combines the elements of fire and water without relying on electricity, gas, or solar energy. Designed around a patented stainless steel wood heater, this shower can produce hot water in as little as three minutes using only a few logs of wood. Water is supplied via a standard garden hose, and a mixing valve allows users to set their preferred temperature, from cold to comfortably warm. Standing over eight feet tall with a minimalist design, the unit is freestanding and can be placed on stone slabs, making it portable and adaptable to various outdoor settings such as patios, decks, forest clearings, or cabin spaces. The design intentionally removes digital controls and complexity, emphasizing a direct, sensory connection with nature. The Feuerwasser shower transforms outdoor bathing into a ritual of elemental simplicity, whether rinsing after gardening, enjoying a quiet morning, or soaking under the stars. By

item.title: Nicole ‘Snooki’ Polizzi Shares Early-Stage Cervical Cancer Diagnosis and Urges Routine Screenings
item.description:
Nicole ‘Snooki’ Polizzi, best known for her role on the reality television series Jersey Shore, has revealed that she has been diagnosed with stage 1 cervical cancer. The 38-year-old shared the news in a candid TikTok video, explaining that a recent cone biopsy confirmed early-stage adenocarcinoma. Although the diagnosis was frightening, Polizzi expressed relief that the cancer was detected early, significantly improving her treatment outlook. She acknowledged the emotional weight of hearing the word “cancer” but emphasized gratitude that it was caught in its initial stage.
Polizzi disclosed that she had experienced abnormal Pap smear results for several years, something she now realizes should always be taken seriously. Using her platform to advocate for women’s health, she urged followers not to delay routine screenings, stressing that early detection can prevent the need for more aggressive treatments such as chemotherapy or radiation. According to her, staying proactive with regul

item.title: Serbia Rebalances Its Foreign Policy Amid Western Pressure and Strained Ties with Russia
item.description:
The article examines what it describes as a significant shift in Serbia’s foreign policy, arguing that Belgrade is gradually moving away from Russia under pressure from the European Union and the United States. It frames Serbia’s traditional strategy of “multi-alignment” — balancing relations between East and West — as increasingly untenable in the current geopolitical climate.
According to the report, Serbian Foreign Minister Marko Djuric stated in an interview that Serbia ranks among the leading European contributors of aid to Ukraine. This contrasts with Serbia’s earlier claims of neutrality in the Russia-Ukraine conflict. President Aleksandar Vucic has acknowledged external pressure from European officials, including requests to restrict Russian media outlets such as RT and Sputnik in exchange for progress in EU accession negotiations.
The article highlights Serbian arms manufacturers supplying ammunition components that ultimately reach Ukraine via intermediaries in the Czech Re

item.title: European Activists Who Dedicated Their Lives to the Palestinian Cause
item.description: This documentary episode explores the lives and sacrifices of two European individuals who devoted themselves to supporting the Palestinian cause. Francoise Kesteman, a French nurse and committed communist, worked in Palestinian refugee camps in Lebanon during the late 1970s and early 1980s. She witnessed the displacement of millions of Palestinians and felt compelled to join armed groups resisting Israeli occupation. Her dedication ultimately cost her life in a paramilitary operation in Lebanon in 1984. Franco Fontana, an Italian photographer and cofounder of a Marxist-Leninist political group, used his art and activism to raise awareness about Palestine. He traveled to Palestine and Lebanon as a photojournalist and also joined groups working toward Palestinian liberation. Fontana, facing illness in 2015, returned to a Palestinian refugee camp in Lebanon, honoring his lifelong commitment by choosing to spend his final days among the people he supported, and was buried there according

item.title: US Ambassador Huckabee Says Biblical Borders Would Make Israeli Expansion 'Fine' in Carlson Interview
item.description: In a February 2026 interview with Tucker Carlson, US Ambassador to Israel Mike Huckabee expressed that it would be acceptable for Israel to control territory spanning from the Nile to the Euphrates, as described in biblical promises to Abraham. This vast area would include modern Lebanon, Syria, Jordan, and parts of other nations. Huckabee stated, “It would be fine if they took it all,” though he noted Israel has no current intention to conquer the region and later called his remark hyperbolic. He suggested that defensive victories leading to territorial gains would warrant discussion. As a Christian Zionist, Huckabee rooted his views in scripture while defending Israel's actions and criticizing international courts like the ICJ and ICC as biased or rogue, supporting efforts to challenge them. The article highlights contradictions with international law, which prohibits acquiring land by force, and references Israel's occupations (Golan Heights, Lebanese outposts) and domestic calls fo

item.title: The Life and Legacy of Józef Antos’ Unique Wind Turbine in Poland
item.description: Józef Antos, born in 1932 in southern Poland, dedicated his life to the ambitious goal of producing electricity from wind for his country. After graduating from a local mining school, he became a skilled autodidact, opening a workshop where he built and repaired machinery, vehicles, and custom equipment. Inspired in the late 1970s by an article on wind energy, Antos began designing his own turbines. Over the years, he experimented with a dozen different models before attempting his most ambitious project: a massive wind turbine in the village of Rębielice Królewskie. The turbine featured an enormous 33-meter diameter rotor with nearly 300 blades atop a 50-meter mast, designed to generate electricity even from very light winds. Antos personally crafted each part and even built the tools and machinery needed for construction, completing the turbine in 2003 with the help of a few friends and all his savings. Unfortunately, the turbine never operated successfully due to unrealistic technic

item.title: North Korea Showcases New Multiple-Launch Missile System with Potential Strategic Capabilities
item.description:
North Korea has unveiled a new missile launcher system capable of firing multiple projectiles, according to an announcement made by leader Kim Jong-un and later broadcast by the state media outlet KCNA. The system reportedly launches missiles of approximately 600 millimeters in caliber and is described by the North Korean military as a cornerstone of the country’s defense strategy. Officials characterized the weapon as suitable for “strategic operations,” a term often associated with nuclear capability, although Pyongyang maintains that the system is intended for defensive and deterrent purposes.
The launcher is said to be capable of firing several missiles in rapid succession, a feature that could complicate interception efforts by missile defense systems deployed in South Korea, Japan, or other neighboring countries. State media emphasized what it called the system’s unprecedented precision and its ability to strike specific targets at considerable range. The proximity of Seoul—loca

item.title: Claims About Epstein, Gates and ResearchGate’s Funding and Influence on Scientific Publishing
item.description:
The article alleges that Jeffrey Epstein and Bill Gates played a significant role in financing ResearchGate, an academic social network where researchers share papers and collaborate. It argues that the platform was developed as a for-profit enterprise designed not only to generate revenue but also to control the flow and visibility of scientific publications through algorithmic influence. According to the text, financial backing from Gates included a $10 million investment in 2013 and participation in later funding rounds totaling tens of millions of dollars. The article also references investment involvement from firms such as Goldman Sachs and Wellcome Trust.
The piece connects Epstein to the venture through documents reportedly found in his files, including references to Biosys, a venture capital firm, and to Boris Nikolic, described as a science advisor to Gates. It claims that emails and agreements from 2013 and 2014 discussed potential financial gains tied to ResearchGate’s gro

item.title: Call for a Permanent and Affordable Public Housing System in Spain
item.description:
The article argues that Spain’s housing crisis is not a temporary problem but a structural consequence of a capitalist economic model in which housing functions primarily as a vehicle for profit. It describes how rising rents, unaffordable mortgages, stagnant wages, and the threat of eviction are pushing working-class families into increasingly precarious living conditions. According to the text, public housing in Spain accounts for only around 2% of the total housing stock, far below the European average, which the author interprets as evidence of a deliberate policy framework designed to encourage speculation and private profit.
The piece criticizes successive governments for promoting market-based solutions that, in the author’s view, fail to address the root of the problem. So-called “affordable housing” initiatives are described as misleading, since they often involve public land transferred to private developers, rents only slightly below market levels, and time-limited protecti

item.title: Mullvad's Anti-Censorship Ads Banned on UK Television Amid Rising Surveillance
item.description: Mullvad, a VPN provider, faced censorship in the United Kingdom after attempting to broadcast its TV commercial 'And Then?', directed by Jonas Åkerlund, which criticized mass surveillance and censorship. While the campaign aired on major channels in Germany, Sweden, and the US, it was entirely blocked in the UK. Clearcast, responsible for approving TV ads, cited vague reasons such as lack of clarity, potential offense, and irrelevant content, including references to criminal activities and sensitive topics. Mullvad described these justifications as Kafkaesque and Orwellian, emphasizing that they prevent meaningful discussion about government surveillance. In response to the ban, Mullvad attempted underground campaigns using QR codes and alternative graphics, which were also restricted by authorities, including Transport for London. Ultimately, the company resorted to displaying a simplified message, 'And Then?', in underground and later overground advertising units without further oppo

item.title: Exploring Thailand’s Remote Landscapes Through Golf
item.description: Thailand is often associated with bustling Bangkok or picturesque beaches like Phuket, but there is another way to experience the country’s diverse landscapes: golf. Playing golf in rural Thailand offers a slow, immersive journey through mountain valleys, lush jungles, and dramatic coastal cliffs that typical tourists often miss. Courses like Chiang Mai Highlands in the north provide misty mountain mornings and cool temperatures, while Red Mountain Golf Club in Phuket features stunning red rock cliffs and tropical vegetation from a former mining site. Golfing in Thailand is also a cultural experience, as caddies serve as knowledgeable guides, introducing players to local history, wildlife, and cuisine. Services like SawadeeGolf help visitors access hidden courses far from crowded city resorts, offering a unique and peaceful way to explore the country. From northern highlands to central valleys and southern peaks, Thailand’s scenic golf courses combine exercise, adventure, and panoramic

item.title: Building a Startup Entirely on European Infrastructure: Challenges, Trade-offs and Lessons Learned
item.description:
In this article, the author recounts his experience building a startup entirely on European infrastructure rather than relying on dominant American hyperscalers such as AWS. Motivated by concerns about data sovereignty, GDPR compliance, and reducing dependency on US-based tech giants, he set out to prove that a fully European stack was viable. What he discovered was that while it is possible, it requires deliberate effort and a willingness to accept trade-offs.
His final stack includes Hetzner for core compute, Scaleway for complementary services like transactional email and container registry, Bunny.net for CDN and edge services, Nebius for AI inference workloads, and Hanko for authentication. He also self-hosts several tools on Kubernetes using Rancher, including Gitea for source control, Plausible for analytics, and other services for CRM, secrets management, and error tracking. This approach gives him tighter control over data and costs, but increases operational responsibility.

item.title: NASA Reports Serious Safety Issues in Starliner CFT Crewed Mission
item.description: The Starliner CFT mission, Boeing's first crewed flight of its Starliner spacecraft, faced significant safety issues, classified as a 'Type A Incident' by NASA—the agency's most severe category, used for missions like Challenger and Columbia, though without loss of life. Launched on June 5, 2024, the Starliner Calypso experienced thruster problems during ISS docking, forcing an uncrewed return on September 7, 2024. The crew, Butch Wilmore and Sunita Williams, returned months later via SpaceX's Crew-9. The mission, initially expected to last 10–30 days, extended to 286 days. Previous Starliner test flights (OFT-1 and OFT-2) also faced serious issues, including thruster failures, valve malfunctions, and excessive propellant usage. During CFT, helium leaks and overheating caused five of 28 maneuvering thrusters to fail temporarily, risking loss of control. Although the spacecraft eventually docked successfully, the incident revealed systemic problems in Boeing's quality management and NAS

item.title: Georgia Elections Board Reprimands Elon Musk’s PAC for Sending Prefilled Ballot Applications
item.description: The Georgia State Elections Board has formally reprimanded Elon Musk's America PAC after discovering that it sent prefilled absentee ballot applications to voters in violation of state law. The law prohibits anyone other than an authorized relative from sending completed absentee ballot forms to electors. Additionally, the PAC failed to clearly indicate that the materials were not official government documents. This action comes in the context of aggressive campaigning during the 2024 elections, where Georgia played a critical role as a battleground state. The investigation began in October 2024 following numerous complaints from residents across multiple counties. The board's decision to issue a formal letter of reprimand underscores the seriousness of the violation. While other news sections of the source article discuss various controversies involving former President Donald Trump, including disputes over military action in Iran, Supreme Court rulings on tariffs, and issues with vet

item.title: Trump reacts strongly after Supreme Court blocks his tariff authority
item.description: President Donald Trump reacted angrily after the U.S. Supreme Court ruled 6-3 that he exceeded his authority when imposing sweeping tariffs under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA). The ruling declared a significant portion of Trump's tariffs unlawful. During a White House address, Trump criticized the Court's decision, stating he could destroy trade and countries but could not collect even a one-dollar tariff. Trump emphasized that his tariff policy has been central to his presidency, warning that losing it could trigger a $2 trillion economic impact and diminish the United States' global status. Despite the ruling, Trump indicated he plans to pursue alternative tariffs, claiming they could generate more revenue. The episode highlights tensions between the executive branch and the judiciary, as well as the potential impact of Trump's economic policies on international trade relationships.

item.title: Iran, China, and Russia Formalize Strategic Cooperation Without Military Alliance
item.description: On February 2, 2026, Iran, China, and Russia signed a new strategic agreement that strengthens their diplomatic, economic, and security coordination. Unlike previous bilateral agreements—such as Iran-Russia's 20-year partnership and Iran-China's 25-year cooperation deal—this pact formally connects all three nations in a structured framework. Officials emphasized that the agreement focuses on respecting national sovereignty, enhancing economic collaboration, and coordinating strategic policies, while deliberately avoiding the formation of a mutual defense or military alliance. The countries also collectively voiced opposition to renewed sanctions targeting Iran over its nuclear program, using the pact as a means to increase their diplomatic leverage amid escalating tensions with the United States. State media in each country framed the pact as a step toward a new multipolar global order, signaling a shift in international alignments. Analysts note that while the pact stops short of mili

item.title: Hayden Clay Creates Geometric Cloud Forms and Dreamlike Neighborhoods in Pastel Digital Art
item.description: Artist Hayden Clay reimagines everyday elements like clouds in unexpected geometric shapes, departing from traditional fluffy, organic forms to present soft, floaty versions structured as perfect cubes and rectangles. This distinctive approach extends beyond clouds to include houses and entire neighborhoods rendered in a consistent pastel color palette that evokes a dreamlike atmosphere. Clay's style skillfully blends serenity with subtle disorientation, producing an emotional effect often described as calm unease that invites viewers to question familiar perceptions of reality. The works feature minimalist compositions where these unnatural yet gentle forms float in expansive, tranquil spaces, enhancing the surreal quality. Available on his official website, Clay offers prints of these pieces for purchase, allowing collectors to bring home elements of his imaginative worlds. His art fits within contemporary digital and 3D-influenced surrealism, drawing from natural themes while introd

item.title: Allegation of ICE Agents Assaulting U.S. Citizen Sparks Outrage Online
item.description:
A post published by Raw America on Substack alleges that a U.S. citizen was violently confronted by individuals identified as Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents. According to the account, the woman was followed by an unmarked vehicle, boxed in while driving, forcibly removed from her car, and left injured in a parking lot after agents reportedly discovered her American passport. The post claims she was on her way to pay rent and purchase a birthday cake for her grandson when the incident occurred. As described, she sustained a torn rotator cuff, a concussion, and bruised ribs. The narrative emphasizes that she received no assistance or explanation following the encounter.
The language used in the post characterizes the event as an example of 'lawless violence' rather than legitimate law enforcement activity. It urges readers to share the story widely to raise awareness, suggesting that similar incidents may be affecting ordinary people. However, the post provides no cor

item.title: China recomienda a sus ciudadanos abandonar Irán por el aumento de tensiones con Estados Unidos
item.description: El Gobierno chino ha instado a sus ciudadanos a abandonar Irán de inmediato debido al aumento de las tensiones en la región, especialmente por la presencia militar de Estados Unidos y las amenazas del presidente Donald Trump. El Ministerio de Exteriores chino ha advertido sobre un aumento significativo de los riesgos de seguridad, destacando la posibilidad de un ataque limitado de Estados Unidos. En respuesta, las autoridades iraníes han alertado que cualquier acción de este tipo sería considerada como un acto de agresión que provocaría una respuesta militar. China también ha desaconsejado viajar a Irán y ha ofrecido asistencia a sus nacionales para abandonar el país, ya sea mediante vuelos comerciales o por pasos terrestres. La situación está marcada por la reciente ronda de negociaciones nucleares entre Irán y Estados Unidos, que se celebraron en Ginebra, aunque la relación sigue siendo tensa debido a los bombardeos israelíes y estadounidenses de 2025.

item.title: Idealista manipula los precios de alquiler para inflar el mercado inmobiliario
item.description: El portal inmobiliario Idealista ha sido acusado de distorsionar los precios de alquiler mediante la publicación de anuncios que están muy por encima de los precios reales de los contratos. Un estudio reciente reveló que la diferencia entre el precio de oferta y el precio real es del 44%, especialmente en la compra de viviendas. Este fenómeno se ha dado en varias ciudades españolas, como Barcelona, donde se ha observado que el precio medio por metro cuadrado en Idealista es un 65,5% superior al precio real. Estos portales, como Idealista, no solo muestran precios inexactos, sino que además influyen en las decisiones de los arrendadores e inmobiliarias, quienes podrían aumentar sus precios basándose en estos anuncios. Esta manipulación del mercado ha llevado al Ministerio de Consumo a abrir una investigación. Las autoridades también han señalado que estas plataformas tienen incentivos perversos para inflar los precios, ya que una menor rotación de alquileres beneficia a su negocio. La f

item.title: La Casa del Rey confirma que el regreso de Juan Carlos I depende de su residencia fiscal en España
item.description: La Casa del Rey ha confirmado en un comunicado oficial que no existe impedimento para el regreso definitivo de Juan Carlos I a España, pero ha subrayado que, para salvaguardar su imagen y la de la institución monárquica, el exmonarca debería recuperar su residencia fiscal en el país. El regreso del padre del rey Felipe VI, autoexiliado en Abu Dabi desde 2020, se debate desde hace meses, y la Casa Real ha dejado claro que la decisión depende de Juan Carlos I. Además, se le ha recordado que no podría residir en el Palacio de la Zarzuela, lo que complica sus planes de estancia. Aunque el rey emérito ha expresado que no tiene intención de regresar de forma definitiva, planea aumentar la frecuencia de sus visitas temporales. Sin embargo, su deseo de mantener su residencia fiscal en Abu Dabi podría generar fricciones con la postura de su hijo, el rey Felipe VI.

item.title: V Jornada Doctoral REDINTUR-ICÁRION 2026: Investigación y colaboración en turismo
item.description: La Red Interuniversitaria de Posgrados en Turismo (REDINTUR) ha anunciado la celebración de la V Jornada Doctoral REDINTUR-ICÁRION, que se llevará a cabo el 16 de abril de 2026 en la Facultat de Turisme de la Universitat de Girona. Este evento, de carácter anual, se consolida como un espacio clave para la investigación en turismo, reuniendo a doctorandos, profesores y expertos del sector turístico. En esta jornada se presentarán avances de tesis doctorales, se celebrarán mesas de debate con investigadores consolidados y se entregará el I Premio a la mejor tesis doctoral en turismo REDINTUR-ICÁRION. La jornada también fomentará la transferencia de conocimiento entre la universidad y el sector profesional, destacando la importancia de generar redes de cooperación científica y profesional. Con el apoyo de entidades internacionales como Switzerland Tourism y Switzerland Travel, el evento se posiciona como una plataforma que promueve la investigación innovadora y sostenible en turismo. Este

item.title: El bot de nutrición Grok de Elon Musk da recomendaciones absurdas sobre el uso rectal de vegetales
item.description: El Gobierno de EE.UU. ha implementado el chatbot Grok, creado por Elon Musk, como parte de sus nuevas guías dietéticas centradas en la proteína. Sin embargo, tras probarlo, se descubrió que Grok hace recomendaciones inapropiadas, como sugerir vegetales para ser insertados en el recto, lo cual pone en duda la eficacia y seriedad de la herramienta. Aunque la página oficial ha eliminado las menciones directas a Grok, el chatbot sigue siendo utilizado en el sitio web del Gobierno, con recomendaciones sobre nutrición. Esta situación plantea interrogantes sobre las implicaciones de utilizar inteligencia artificial en políticas públicas y la calidad de las herramientas tecnológicas implementadas en el sector gubernamental.

item.title: Confinamiento en Muskiz debido a una fuga de benceno en la refinería
item.description: Una fuga de benceno en la refinería de Petronor, ubicada en Muskiz, ha generado niveles elevados de este compuesto en el aire, alcanzando entre 100 y 200 microgramos por metro cúbico. Debido a los riesgos para la salud, el Gobierno Vasco ha solicitado a los residentes que permanezcan dentro de sus casas con puertas y ventanas cerradas. El benceno, un compuesto volátil derivado de la gasolina, es conocido por ser perjudicial para la salud en concentraciones elevadas. Las autoridades han activado los protocolos de emergencia y están evaluando la situación continuamente. También se han identificado aumentos de otros hidrocarburos como tolueno y xileno en el aire, lo que ha aumentado la preocupación entre los vecinos. La empresa responsable de la refinería asegura que está trabajando para resolver el problema lo antes posible. El confinamiento se mantendrá hasta que los niveles de contaminación regresen a niveles seguros.

item.title: Libertad Digital difundió información sobre supuesta enfermedad cardiovascular de Pedro Sánchez, desmentida por el presidente, tras recibir más de un millón en publicidad institucional de Ayuso y Almeida en 2024
item.description: El grupo mediático Libertad Digital, presidido por Federico Jiménez Losantos, publicó una información afirmando que Pedro Sánchez padece una lesión cardiovascular tratada en secreto desde hace meses en el Hospital Ramón y Cajal de Madrid por el doctor Zamorano, con riesgos potenciales como infartos o trombosis si no se controla. El periodista Miguel Ángel Pérez fue quien difundió esta noticia en artículo y vídeo. Tanto el Gobierno como el propio presidente Sánchez han desmentido categóricamente estos hechos. Sánchez publicó en X que no sufre ninguna enfermedad cardiovascular, aunque añadió que, de padecerla, no sería problema y millones de personas conviven con dolencias similares gracias a la sanidad pública. Acusó a la derecha y ultraderecha de usar bulos lanzados desde 'pseudomedios', amplificados por diputados del PP como Cayetana Álvarez de Toledo y tertulianos, para atacar al Gobierno. El artículo de El Plural destaca que Libertad Digital recibió cuantiosas cantidades en publicid

item.title: Una guía visual para identificar las eras de los barcos europeos
item.description: Este artículo es una introducción para los aficionados a la historia de los barcos europeos, describiendo cómo identificar las diferentes eras de la navegación desde la Edad Media hasta la Revolución Industrial. Comienza con los barcos vikingos del año 1000, que tenían un solo mástil y velas rectangulares, y sigue con los barcos de la Edad de la Exploración, como los carracks y caravels, con tres mástiles y velas triangulares. También aborda la época de los galeones, los barcos de línea, y las transiciones hacia el uso de la vela combinada con el vapor. A lo largo del artículo, el autor utiliza humor para describir la evolución de los barcos y su influencia en la historia naval. Este tipo de guía visual es útil para principiantes que desean comprender mejor la evolución de los barcos en Europa, destacando las características clave de cada era. Con un enfoque entretenido y accesible, se invita a los lectores a sumergirse en la rica historia marítima de Europa.

item.title: El Baskonia homenajea a las víctimas de la masacre del 3 de marzo de 1976 en su 50 aniversario
item.description: El pasado 26 de febrero de 2026, el Saski Baskonia organizó un emotivo acto de homenaje en el Buesa Arena de Vitoria-Gasteiz con motivo del 50.º aniversario de la masacre del 3 de marzo de 1976, en la que cinco obreros —Bienvenido Perea, Francisco Aznar, Romualdo Barroso, Pedro M. Martínez y José Castillo— perdieron la vida por disparos de la Policía Armada durante el desalojo de una asamblea obrera en la iglesia de San Francisco de Asís, en el barrio de Zaramaga. Los familiares de las víctimas fueron recibidos en primer lugar por miembros de la peña Indar Baskonia, quienes les entregaron bufandas conmemorativas y cuadros con los rostros de los fallecidos. Posteriormente, el presidente del club, Josean Kerejeta, les hizo entrega de camisetas personalizadas del equipo con el nombre y la edad de cada víctima en el dorsal. Durante el partido de Liga contra el Valencia Basket (que terminó con derrota local por 79-109), en el descanso los familiares saltaron a la cancha con dichas camisetas

item.title: Análisis comparativo de las economías de Europa y EE. UU. tras la crisis de 2008
item.description: Paul Krugman, economista de renombre, ofrece un análisis detallado sobre las economías de Europa y Estados Unidos, comparando sus rendimientos desde 2007 hasta 2024. A través de tres gráficos, Krugman cuestiona la sabiduría convencional que sostiene que Europa va a la zaga de EE. UU. en términos económicos. Destaca que, aunque el Producto Interior Bruto (PIB) de EE. UU. creció más rápido que el de la UE, esta diferencia puede estar influenciada por la fluctuación del euro frente al dólar y las variaciones en los precios relativos de los bienes. Además, explica cómo la concentración de la industria tecnológica en EE. UU. ha disparado su crecimiento, mientras que Europa mantiene su sector no tecnológico sin grandes cambios. Krugman subraya que este análisis puede ser erróneo si no se consideran factores como los precios de compra ajustados y las diferencias en la producción de bienes. A pesar de las diferencias de crecimiento, el economista afirma que la calidad de vida en Europa sigue s

item.title: La conexión entre la élite empresarial y la política de Trump: un análisis sobre el poder en Hollywood y Silicon Valley
item.description: El artículo de Roger Senserrich analiza la creciente influencia de grandes empresarios como Larry Ellison en la política estadounidense, particularmente en su relación con el expresidente Donald Trump. Destaca cómo Ellison, fundador de Oracle, ha transitado desde un donante bipartisan hasta un aliado cercano de Trump. Además, se discute la compra de Paramount por parte de su hijo David Ellison, vinculado con la administración Trump, lo que refleja la influencia de los intereses empresariales sobre los medios de comunicación. También se aborda cómo el trumpismo afecta a los medios y cómo las grandes empresas tecnológicas y cinematográficas se entrelazan con el poder político. La concentración de poder económico y mediático parece fortalecer el control de una oligarquía empresarial sobre la política, mientras que Trump sigue alimentando su narrativa en defensa de los intereses de la élite empresarial, especialmente en un contexto de declive de medios tradicionales como CNN.

item.title: La exjefa de prensa de Mazón reconoce ante la jueza que las informaciones iniciales sobre su ubicación durante la DANA se basaron en suposiciones y no en hechos
item.description: Maite Gómez, que fue jefa de prensa y exdirectora general de la Oficina de Prensa de la Presidencia de la Generalitat Valenciana bajo Carlos Mazón, declaró como testigo ante la jueza que investiga la gestión de la DANA del 29 de octubre de 2024, que causó 230 fallecidos. Gómez admitió que las versiones facilitadas a los medios en los días posteriores a la catástrofe sobre los horarios y movimientos de Mazón no se basaron en datos reales, sino en horas que 'parecieron lógicas' al equipo de Presidencia. La jueza calificó esta explicación como 'muy poco creíble'. La testigo explicó que fue convocada a las 19:45 para acompañar al president al Cecopi y se incorporó al coche oficial alrededor de las 20:08 en las Torres de Serranos, sin conocer con exactitud la hora de llegada de Mazón al Palau tras su comida en el restaurante El Ventorro. Negó haber escuchado conversaciones relevantes sobre la emergencia durante el trayecto y afirmó que gestionó la anulación de unos billetes de AVE para un a

item.title: El PPCV recoloca a los principales colaboradores de Carlos Mazón en instituciones públicas tras su salida del Palau
item.description: Tres meses después de la salida de Carlos Mazón de la presidencia de la Generalitat Valenciana, sus colaboradores más cercanos han encontrado acomodo en diferentes entidades bajo el paraguas de la administración pública o del PPCV. El último caso es el de Santiago Lumbreras, ex mano derecha de Mazón en varios cargos y exsecretario autonómico de Relaciones Institucionales y Transparencia, quien se incorpora como asesor en el grupo popular de la Federación Valenciana de Municipios y Provincias (FVMP) con un sueldo de 32.804 euros anuales, según la resolución de compatibilidad de la Generalitat. Otros miembros del círculo de confianza también han sido recolocados: José Manuel Cuenca, exjefe de gabinete, ocupa un puesto en la Oficina del Expresident con 58.000 euros al año; Cayetano García pasa a ser secretario autonómico en Hacienda; Francisco González dirige Promoción Institucional; y Maite Gómez permanece como asesora en Presidencia. Ruth Merino, exconsellera de Hacienda, ha sido nombra

item.title: Detenido un ladrón que sustrajo dos perros de una protectora en Badalona, vendiendo uno y abandonando el otro
item.description: El pasado domingo 23 de febrero, un individuo forzó la valla y la puerta del Centre d'Acollida d'Animals de Companyia del Barcelonès Nord (CAACBN) en Badalona, llevándose a dos perros, Tyson, un cruce de pitbull de un año, y Zander, un cachorro de tres meses mezcla de pastor belga con pitbull. Inicialmente se pensó que se trataba de un robo común, pero pronto se descubrió que el objetivo del ladrón era lucrarse con los animales. Tras la denuncia presentada a la Guàrdia Urbana de Badalona y la difusión en redes sociales, Tyson apareció deambulando solo por la ciudad, mientras la búsqueda de Zander continuaba. Finalmente, gracias a la colaboración de vecinos que identificaron al perro, se localizó a Zander en compañía de una pareja que lo había adquirido al ladrón, quien previamente había grabado un vídeo mostrando todos los perros disponibles. La policía catalana, a través de los Mossos d'Esquadra, ha iniciado una investigación para esclarecer los hechos, imputar posibles delitos de sus

item.title: Absueltos los exdirigentes de la CEP en Valencia por falta de pruebas en la gestión de gastos sindicales
item.description:
La jueza de la Sección Penal del Tribunal de Instancia de Valencia ha absuelto a los dos máximos responsables de los comités regional y provincial de la Confederación Española de Policía (CEP) en la Comunitat Valenciana, acusados de administración desleal por supuestamente malversar más de 10.000 euros en fondos sindicales. Los gastos cuestionados incluían comidas, cenas, cafés, almuerzos y kilometrajes realizados entre finales de 2018 y 2019, que la acusación particular cifró en 11.102 euros y consideró excesivos e injustificados tras una auditoría interna del sindicato.
La sentencia destaca que no existían criterios unificados ni una normativa clara en la gestión económica de los comités, y que la mayoría de los gastos estaban autorizados mediante actas. La magistrada concluye que no se ha acreditado un exceso consciente e intencionado más allá de lo razonable en sus facultades de administración. Los acusados defendieron que se trataba de gastos habituales derivados de su labor sind

item.title: Trabajadoras de residencias de mayores de La Rioja expulsadas del Parlamento durante sus reivindicaciones
item.description: Las trabajadoras de residencias y centros de día de personas mayores en La Rioja han intensificado sus protestas por la falta de mejoras en sus condiciones laborales. Este miércoles, varias de ellas acudieron al pleno del Parlamento regional para visibilizar su situación, pero fueron obligadas a entrar con las camisetas del sindicato tapadas y, finalmente, expulsadas por la presidenta del pleno al reclamar directamente a la consejera de Salud, María Martín, que las condiciones de trabajo deben mejorar. El conflicto se centra en la ratio insuficiente de personal: La Rioja cumple con la ratio regional de 0,31-0,33 trabajadores por residente, pero no alcanza la recomendación del Gobierno de España de 0,35. Además, las trabajadoras denuncian que la inversión anunciada por el Gobierno no se traduce en mejoras reales para el personal, y critican la falta de inspecciones efectivas y la inacción de la patronal. Desde septiembre, han realizado numerosas concentraciones y manifestaciones, y han

item.title: El CEO de la Universidad Alfonso X, con experiencia previa en consultoría tecnológica para Israel, impulsa la apertura de un campus en Oviedo
item.description: Domingo Mirón, actual consejero delegado del Grupo Educativo de la Universidad Alfonso X el Sabio (UAX), ha anunciado la apertura en septiembre de 2026 de los grados de Medicina y Enfermería en un nuevo campus en Oviedo/Uviéu. La iniciativa ha generado polémica política y sindical, con apoyo parcial del Gobierno asturiano y rechazo de IU-Convocatoria por Asturies, CCOO e Intersindical Asturiana. Mirón posee más de 35 años de experiencia en alta dirección, incluyendo su reciente etapa como presidente de Iberia e Israel de Accenture hasta 2023. Durante su tiempo en Accenture, una firma internacional de consultoría tecnológica, trabajó con gobiernos, entre ellos el de Israel, participando en el desarrollo de sistemas de vigilancia y ciberseguridad utilizados tanto internamente como en territorios palestinos ocupados. Estas tecnologías incluyen herramientas de policía predictiva y reconocimiento facial, denunciadas por organizaciones de derechos humanos como Amnistía Internacional por rest

item.title: Hillary Clinton testifica ante el Congreso por el caso Epstein y exige lo mismo a Trump
item.description: Hillary Clinton, exsecretaria de Estado de Estados Unidos, testificó el jueves ante el Comité de Supervisión de la Cámara de Representantes, controlado por los republicanos, sobre sus presuntos vínculos con Jeffrey Epstein, el financiero fallecido en 2019 mientras esperaba juicio por delitos sexuales. Durante su comparecencia a puerta cerrada, Clinton exigió que el presidente Donald Trump también declare bajo juramento, argumentando que él también aparece en documentos relacionados con Epstein. Su esposo, el expresidente Bill Clinton, está citado para declarar el viernes siguiente bajo amenaza de desacato al Congreso. Inicialmente, los Clinton rechazaron las citaciones, proponiendo alternativas como declaraciones juradas o entrevistas transcritas, pero finalmente accedieron ante la presión republicana liderada por James Comer, presidente del comité. Tanto republicanos como algunos demócratas apoyaron las citaciones, aunque los Clinton denunciaron motivaciones partidistas para proteger

item.title: Investigación en Defensa por contratos adjudicados a una tienda de motos para material crítico de aeronaves
item.description: Recientemente, se ha descubierto que una pequeña tienda de motos en España obtuvo contratos para suministrar material técnico para los cazas Eurofighter y los aviones contraincendios CL-215T y CL-415. La adjudicación, realizada por la Base Aérea de Albacete, se produjo con una diferencia de apenas 1.405 euros frente a un fabricante especializado, lo que ha levantado sospechas sobre la transparencia del proceso y posibles irregularidades. Entre los suministros se incluyen escaleras móviles con plataformas adaptadas al fuselaje de aeronaves y fabricadas con aleaciones de alta resistencia, equipos cuya fiabilidad es crítica para la seguridad de vuelo. Además, se han señalado presuntas irregularidades en la Maestranza Aérea de Albacete, como el uso de instalaciones y personal civil para trabajos de interés privado, traslado de piezas a espacios no oficiales y contratos con poca competencia. Todo esto plantea serias dudas sobre los mecanismos de control en la contratación de Defensa en Espa

item.title: ¿Quieres que te lo cuente? (curta)
item.description: Se trata de un cortometraje español (curta significa corto en catalán/gallego/portugués) subido hace muchos años, probablemente una pieza humorística, de terror o con un giro inesperado, presentada de forma misteriosa con el título que invita a ver el vídeo para descubrir la historia.

item.title: Bigote Arrocet relata cómo conoció al novio de Ayuso durante la venta de la casa de María Teresa Campos
item.description: María Teresa Campos logró vender finalmente en 2021 su mansión de Las Rozas, tras seis años de intentos fallidos, en los que vivió con Edmundo Arrocet, conocido como Bigote, durante su relación de seis años. Durante este periodo, ambos se reunieron en varias ocasiones con Alberto González Amador, novio de Isabel Díaz Ayuso, con la intención de que les ayudara a encontrar un comprador para la propiedad. Sin embargo, según relata Arrocet, aunque hubo reuniones y comidas con González Amador, la ayuda no fructificó y la venta no se concretó hasta después de que él y Campos terminaran su relación a finales de 2019. Arrocet recuerda su primer encuentro con González Amador en un contexto profesional, en el que ambos compartieron contactos y experiencias de negocios en Latinoamérica, donde González Amador realizaba auditorías y cursos de formación para empresas. El humorista chileno describe al empresario como una persona amable, trabajadora y con buenos contactos, aunque señala que no mantien

item.title: HOMBRE PERFORMATIVO
item.description: El vídeo muestra a un hombre realizando una performance artística en la calle, donde se queda completamente inmóvil adoptando poses extrañas, interactuando visualmente con los transeúntes. La performance parece explorar temas de identidad, percepción y la relación entre el individuo y el entorno.

item.title: La Audiencia de Pontevedra ordena reabrir la denuncia de una doctora víctima de maltrato durante décadas
item.description: La Audiencia Provincial de Pontevedra ha ordenado reabrir la denuncia presentada por una médica de familia de Vigo, que había sido archivada por el Juzgado de Violencia sobre la Mujer número 1 de la ciudad. El juez instructor, Pablo de San Mateo, había cerrado la causa argumentando que la violencia de género no podía afectar a cualquier mujer y cuestionando la tardanza en la denuncia de la víctima. La doctora denunciaba décadas de maltrato por parte de su exmarido, desde 1990 hasta 2021, incluyendo agresión sexual, lesiones psicológicas, violencia económica, control sobre sus movimientos y aislamiento social. La Audiencia critica que no se tomara declaración a testigos clave, como el hijo de la víctima, ni se practicaran diligencias mínimas de investigación. Además, se han presentado informes psicológicos que vinculan directamente los problemas de salud mental de la mujer con el maltrato sufrido, justificando la demora en interponer la denuncia. Este fallo judicial evidencia los prejui

item.title: Evolución del comercio mundial: China supera a EE. UU. y la UE en 2024
item.description: La dinámica del comercio global ha experimentado cambios significativos entre 2000 y 2024. En el año 2000, Estados Unidos era el socio comercial dominante en gran parte de América y en la región Asia-Pacífico, mientras que la Unión Europea lideraba en Europa, partes de África y algunos países de Sudamérica. China, en aquel entonces, tenía un papel limitado, siendo principal socio solo para unos pocos países asiáticos cercanos. En 2024, la situación ha cambiado radicalmente: China se ha consolidado como el mayor socio comercial de numerosos países de Asia, África y Sudamérica, aumentando su comercio total de 474.000 millones de dólares en 2000 a 6,2 billones en 2024. Estados Unidos mantiene su influencia en Norteamérica y algunas regiones de Latinoamérica, y la UE sigue liderando en Europa y parte del norte de África, pero ambos han perdido terreno frente a China, especialmente en el Sur Global. Estos cambios no solo reflejan cifras de comercio, sino también nuevas relaciones económicas

item.title: Paramount Skydance comprará Warner Bros tras la retirada de Netflix
item.description: Tras meses de intensa competencia por adquirir los históricos estudios de Warner Bros, Paramount Skydance está a punto de hacerse con la compañía tras la decisión de Netflix de no igualar la última oferta. Los ejecutivos de Netflix, Ted Sarandos y Greg Peters, declararon que la operación ya no resultaba financieramente atractiva al nivel de la propuesta de Paramount. La oferta de Paramount, liderada por Larry Ellison y su hijo David, asciende a más de 111.000 millones de dólares, frente a los 83.000 millones de la propuesta de Netflix, incluyendo un precio por acción superior y tarifas adicionales por retrasos regulatorios. La transacción excluye algunos negocios de televisión por cable de Warner y el universo de entretenimiento de Discovery, pero cubre la mayoría de los activos de cine y streaming, incluyendo HBO Max y grandes éxitos de taquilla. Warner Bros Discovery avaló que la propuesta de Paramount era superior, permitiendo romper el preacuerdo con Netflix y abrir la negociación

item.title: El Departamento de Justicia retira y restaura una foto de Howard Lutnick en los archivos relacionados con Epstein
item.description:
Una fotografía difundida por el Departamento de Justicia como parte de los denominados archivos de Epstein, en la que aparece el actual secretario de Comercio Howard Lutnick junto a Jeffrey Epstein y otros tres hombres en la isla privada del financiero en el Caribe, fue retirada temporalmente del sitio web oficial del organismo antes de ser restaurada horas después. La imagen, que ha sido autenticada por CBS News, fue descargada y archivada por plataformas como la Wayback Machine a finales de enero. En un primer momento, el enlace donde estaba alojada la fotografía mostró un mensaje de página no encontrada tras su eliminación.
La publicación de la imagen forma parte de la amplia divulgación de documentos relacionados con Epstein. Entre los nuevos archivos figuran correos electrónicos que muestran que Lutnick y su familia planearon una visita a la isla en 2012, incluyendo un almuerzo en Nochebuena. Durante una comparecencia ante el Congreso, Lutnick reconoció haber estado en la isla co

item.title: El Congreso aprueba la revalorización de las pensiones con el rechazo de Vox
item.description:
El Congreso de los Diputados ha aprobado la revalorización de las pensiones en un segundo intento parlamentario, después de que la medida fuese rechazada previamente al incluirse en el denominado decreto “ómnibus”. En esta ocasión, la subida se ha presentado de forma separada y ha contado con el respaldo mayoritario de la Cámara, quedando Vox como único partido que ha votado en contra.
